data.title: Tesla dips ahead of earnings as investors eye margins update, deliveries guidance
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) was down 1% in afternoon trading on Tuesday as investors prepared for the company's Q3 earnings report on Wednesday after the closing bell.

As for Q3 numbers, analysts expect the EV maker to post revenue of $25.7 billion and EPS of $0.60. The operating margin line will be closely watched after Tesla (TSLA) saw a sequential improvement in Q2 to 6.3%. The consensus estimate for Q3 is 8.0%, which would be an improvement from last year's mark of 7.7%.

Elon Musk's commentary on the current quarter could be critical, as analysts forecast about 496,000 deliveries for the quarter. Tesla (TSLA) would have to deliver about 515,000 vehicles in Q4 to pull the 2024 total above last year's level. The consensus estimate for 2025 deliveries is 2 million vehicles.

On the conference call, Elon Musk may once again skip over details about the Model 2 after omitting mention of it during the robotaxi event on October 14. "My sense is that the car is still on the roadmap for some time late in 2025, but the company decided not to discuss it, as it would have triggered the Osborne Effect, causing Model 3 buyers to hold off," highlighted Deepwater Asset Management's Gene Munster. The Osborne effect referenced by Munster is a social phenomenon where the premature announcement of a future product negatively impacts the sales of a company's current product. On the robotaxi front, Tesla (TSLA) is aiming for a CyberCab introduction by 2027 at a $30,000 price point.

On Seeking Alpha, analyst Brian Gilmartin said investor expectations are low for the Tesla (TSLA) Q3 earnings. Gilmartin has a Hold rating on TSLA, with investors' sentiment on the EV stock low. "Trading at 100x EPS today, and 60x cash flow, the few positive developments seem to have been overwhelmed with the stagnant robocar division and the delayed rollout of the lower-end model, but Tesla has much easier compares ahead," he observed.

data.title: Staples continued its selling streak, the longest among sectors last week
data.description:
The consumer staples sector (NYSEARCA:XLP) has the longest recent selling streak at four weeks of outflows.

BofA analysts are underweight the sector due to it being lower quality than history and having weak guidance trends, said Jill Hall, head of U.S. small/mid-cap strategy at BofA, in a note.

Consumer staples (XLP) has seen $1.37B of outflows so far this year, after seeing $655M of outflows in 2023, and $293M of inflows the year before.

For the week beginning Oct. 14, the sector saw $519M of outflows. The week prior, it saw $77M in outflows.

However, ETFs did see some inflows, staying in the positive.

data.title: Highest quant rated real estate stocks, as sector sees first week of inflows in +2 months
data.description:
Real Estate (NYSEARCA:XLRE) saw its first week of inflows in nine weeks, according to BofA data.

The sector was also one of the three sectors seeing inflows this week, with communication services (XLC) and materials (XLB) heading the list.

Last week, real estate (XLRE) saw $119M in inflows, compared to $36M of outflows the previous week, and $258M of outflows the week prior.

Cumulatively, however, real estate (XLRE) has seen $1.6B in outflows so far this year, when last year the sector reported $1.79B in inflows.

Analysts continue to favor real estate for income and quality.

Here are the top quant-rated stocks by Seeking Alpha:

  • CTO Realty Growth Inc. (CTO) – Quant rating: 4.97
  • Orion Office REIT Inc. (ONL) – Quant rating: 4.94
  • Cousins Properties Inc. (CUZ) – Quant rating: 4.91

data.title: Northern Trust in pact with Hamilton Lane for private market data, analytics
data.description:
• Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) agreed to offer Hamilton Lane's (NASDAQ:HLNE) Cobalt LP proprietary software and integrated services to its Northern Trust Asset Servicing clients, the companies said on Tuesday.
  • Under the agreement, Northern Trust (NTRS) will offer clients access to Hamilton Lane's (HLNE) private market data, analytics, and tools through its suite of Northern Trust Total Portfolio Analytics solutions, an ecosystem that integrates technology across the investment lifecycle.
  • Cobalt LP provides private market analytics and pre-commitment research capabilities in a digital turn-key format. In addition, Norther Trust's (NTRS) clients will be able to use Hamilton Lane’s underlying portfolio company data service, which includes the tracking of core portfolio company metrics, company fundamentals, and asset class specific metrics across clients’ private markets portfolios.
  • Hamilton Lane (HLNE) stock dipped 1.0% and Northern Trust (NTRS) stock edged up 0.1% in Tuesday afternoon trading. Northern Trust is scheduled to report Q3 earnings on Wednesday.

data.title: Netflix reportedly closed its studio that was making a multi-platform AAA game
data.description:
According to media reports from Tuesday, Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) has closed one of its gaming studios three years after it first entered the online gaming space. The news was first reported by Game File.

The company has shuttered the studio known as Blue. It hired former Overwatch executive producer Chacko Sonny to run operations and brought on a number of game industry veterans with experience working on high-profile franchises, including Halo and God of War, according to The Verge.

The studio was developing a multi-platform AAA game for an original IP but was closed before the game could be announced or released, the report said.

data.title: Pfizer, Lilly relationships with telehealth platforms draw Sen. Durbin's attention
data.description:
• Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), chairman of the Senate Finance Committee, has sent letters to Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) regarding the two drug giants' relationships with telehealth platforms.
  • Durbin is seeking to find out whether the two pharmaceutical companies are violating federal anti-kickback laws, according to the letters.
  • Both Pfizer and Lilly this year launched websites for consumers to find out about their medications, as well as links to talk to a physician online that can prescribe them and an online pharmacy to get prescriptions filled. Pfizer's is called PfizerForAll, while Lilly's is name LillyDirect.
  • Durbin, along with Sens. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.), and Peter Welch (D-Vt.), argue that these setups are designed to push consumers to particular drugs "and create the potential for inappropriate prescribing that can increase spending for federal health programs."
  • Regarding Pfizer's platform, the senators say the ease of getting meds prescribed "creates the impression that any patient interested in a particular medication can indeed receive it with just a few clicks, and the appearance of Pfizer's approval that these chosen telehealth providers can ensure a patient receives the given medication."

data.title: Activist Legion Partners takes Five9 stake, pushed for board seat, cost cuts - Reuters
data.description:
Activist investor Legion Partners has taken a stake in call center software firm Five9 (NASDAQ:FIVN) and is pushing for a board seat.

The activist is also looking for Five9 (FIVN) to cut costs, according to a Reuters report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Reuters didn't know the size of the stake.

The push from Legion Partners comes after Reuters reported in July that Anson Funds had built up a stake in Five9 (FIVN) and was pushing the company to sell itself. The Anson campaign is being headed up by Sagar Gupta, who formerly worked at Legion Partners.

Five9 declined to comment to Reuters.

In August, Five9 (FIVN) said it was planning to reduce its global workforce by 7% as part of its larger plan to increase shareholder value.

data.title: L'Oréal warns of Chinese ecosystem as Q3 sales miss expectations
data.description:
L'Oréal S.A. (OTCPK:LRLCY) warned that the Chinese ecosystem remains challenging, with declining sales in the region resulting in lower-than-expected sales for the French cosmetics giant in the third quarter ended September 30.

“The situation in the Chinese ecosystem has become even more challenging, but we believe in the future of this market and hope that the governmental stimulus will help improve consumer confidence,” CEO Nicolas Hieronimus said in a statement.

In June, Hieronimus made a similar warning that lingering weakness in China would impact growth in the beauty market.

Although sales growth was achieved in all regions, except North Asia, Q3 results were below expectations except for North American sales which were up 5.2% compared to +3.67% estimates. Sales in Europe increased 5.6% (+6.43% est), and in SAPMENA-SSA (which includes Australia, New Zealand, Thailand, Saudi Arabia, India, Vietnam, and Sub-Saharan Africa) where sales improved 8.0% (+12.4% est).

North Asian sales were down 6.5%, worsening from a 2.4% drop in the previous quarter and missing expectations for a gain of 2.15%.

By division, sales of Professional Products rose 6.1% (est +4.5%), followed by a 5.8% gain in Luxe sales (est +4.43%), 1.4% gain in Consumer Products (est +5.18%), and 0.8% increase in sales of Dermatological Beauty (est +11.2%), resulting in an overall sales increase of 3.4%, missing the Bloomberg estimate of +5.8%. All sales are reported as like-for-like ("LFL").

The results weighed on shares of L'Oréal (OTCPK:LRLCY) along with other names in the sector including Estée Lauder (EL), Coty (COTY), e.l.f. Beauty (ELF), and Ulta (ULTA) all of which are down by 2% to nearly 6%.

data.title: Verizon's capex increase fails to move telecom equipment stocks
data.description:
Verizon (NYSE:VZ) expects to increase its capital expenditures in 2025 above market expectations, but the news failed to move telecom equipment manufacturer stocks on Tuesday.

Verizon projected capital expenditures to range between $17B and $17.5B for all of 2024. It forecasts 2025 capex to range between $17.5B and $18.5B, which was above the estimate of $17.56B.

"I always said that if we see an opportunity where we can grow faster, and we can invest more in capex, we will explain that if we go outside the normal BAU levels," said Verizon CEO Hans Vestberg, during Verizon's third quarter 2024 earnings call on Tuesday.

"But ultimately, if we see opportunities, remember the capital allocation priorities, we spend it in business, but we also want to explain that is something additionally we can get and that we can share with our shareholders," Vestberg added.

Verizon plans to double its subscribers for wireless service over the next four years. It is investing heavily in C-band spectrum for 5G coverage. Vestberg said its revealed capex plans do not include the eventual emergence of 6G, which is not expected to arrive until around 2030.

Verizon shares were down 4% by early afternoon trading on Tuesday. Telecom equipment manufacturers, which look to benefit from Verizon's higher-than-expected 2025 capex, remained mostly muted following the news.

Ericsson (NASDAQ:ERIC) was down 0.2%, Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO) +0.3%, Nokia Oyj (NYSE:NOK) -0.26%, Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) -2.3%, and Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR) -0.1%.

data.title: Braze gets initiated with bullish views at Stifel
data.description:
Stifel began coverage of Braze (NASDAQ:BRZE) with a Buy rating and a $37 target price target.

The New York-based company provides software development kits that automatically manage data ingestion and deliver mobile and web notifications.

Analysts led by J. Lane said that as consumers have increased their digital footprints over the years since the start of mobile and social, and consumer expectations have shifted toward more personalized brand experiences, delivering personalized, mobile, and real-time experiences has become a necessity for successful brand in all industries.

The analysts see Braze as well-positioned to capture share in the customer engagement software space, and believe the company has built a leading-edge multi-channel marketing platform which is difficult to replicate.

In addition, the analysts see several ways Braze is actively lowering customer friction, and think that the company remains in its early days of capturing its addressable market.

The analysts view several things directly benefitting customer engagement providers such as Braze — forced shift from third-party data to first party data, consumer expectations and demands for personalization, digitally-native consumers and generative AI-powered marketing motions, among other things.

Personalized, digital outreach has become critical for organizations, and companies like Braze are directly serving this need, according to the analysts.

Lane and his team believe that market trends have dictated a need for a customer engagement solution like Braze for nearly all business-to-consumer, or B2C, organizations, and believe these trends are becoming increasingly important for the B2B businesses.
